description: Transform Bloom into a social reading club application for philosophy enthusiasts. Focus: authentic virtual in-depth conversations with up to 6 people, Discord-like experience, text always visible, RAG-powered moderator.
targetAudience: Students and early-career professionals interested in philosophy
designInspiration: Dribbble top aesthetics - glassmorphism, smooth animations, premium feel

userStories.title: Set up WebSocket infrastructure for real-time chat
acceptanceCriteria: Add socket.io to server dependencies
Create WebSocket server alongside Express
Support room-based connections (each reading session = 1 room)
Handle connect/disconnect events with user presence
Server starts without errors

userStories.title: Create Room and Session data models
acceptanceCriteria: Define Room type: id, bookId, participants (max 6), createdAt, status
Define Participant type: id, displayName, avatarColor, isHost, joinedAt
Define ChatMessage type: id, participantId, content, timestamp, anchorParagraph?
Create in-memory store for rooms (can migrate to DB later)
Export types for frontend use

userStories.title: Create Room Lobby page with Dribbble aesthetics
acceptanceCriteria: New route /rooms showing available reading sessions
Card-based layout with glassmorphism styling
Each card shows: book title, participant count (X/6), host name
Create Room button with modal for book selection
Join button on each room card
Smooth hover animations and transitions
Responsive design (mobile + desktop)

userStories.title: Build split-view Reading Room layout
acceptanceCriteria: Route /room/:roomId with split-panel layout
Left panel (60%): Book reader with current text
Right panel (40%): Chat interface
Resizable divider between panels
Collapsible chat panel for focused reading
Participant avatars shown at top of chat panel
Mobile: stacked layout with toggle between reader/chat

userStories.title: Implement real-time chat with paragraph anchoring
acceptanceCriteria: Chat messages appear in real-time for all participants
Click paragraph in reader to anchor message to that location
Anchored messages show visual indicator linking to paragraph
Click anchored message scrolls reader to that paragraph
Message input with send button and Enter key support
Typing indicator shows when others are typing
Smooth message animations on arrival

userStories.title: Add participant presence and status
acceptanceCriteria: Show online/offline status for each participant
Display participant's current reading position (paragraph)
Join/leave notifications in chat
Participant list with avatars and names
Host badge for room creator
Max 6 participants enforced on join

userStories.title: Create RAG-powered AI Moderator backend
acceptanceCriteria: Endpoint to generate embeddings for book content
Store embeddings (in-memory or simple file cache)
Query endpoint: given conversation context, retrieve relevant passages
Moderator response endpoint using retrieved context + conversation
Use existing OpenAI integration pattern from server/index.js
Moderator persona: wise facilitator, asks probing questions

userStories.title: Integrate AI Moderator into chat UI
acceptanceCriteria: Summon Moderator button in chat interface
Moderator messages styled distinctly (different avatar/color)
Auto-suggest moderator when conversation stalls (no messages for 2+ min)
Moderator references specific paragraphs (anchored to text)
Loading state while moderator generates response
Rate limit: max 1 moderator call per 30 seconds

userStories.title: Polish visual design to Dribbble-level aesthetics
acceptanceCriteria: Consistent glassmorphism styling throughout
Smooth micro-interactions (button hover, panel transitions)
Premium color palette (deep purples, teals, warm accents)
Custom scrollbar styling
Subtle gradient backgrounds
Typography hierarchy with proper spacing
Dark mode as default with light mode option

userStories.title: Add onboarding flow for new users
acceptanceCriteria: Welcome screen explaining the concept
Quick tutorial on paragraph anchoring
Profile setup: display name, avatar color selection
Interests selection (philosophy branches)
Skip option for returning users
Progress indicators during onboarding
